what_it_does: Renders the in-app design-review studio surface as a three-region layout (Rail, Stage, RhmPanel) using StudioKit primitives, and auto-binds the first reviewable mockup/address from the corpus so the Stage and right-hand-man panel have a locus on load.
observations: Heavy architectural comments frame the file as layer ① (surface skeleton) and layer ⑤ (code-level laws)
Imports StudioKit primitives Rail, Stage, RhmPanel, Composer and uses AppContext
Defines a small titleFor helper that looks up a corpus title by file path
Two useEffects: one refreshes the corpus on mount, the other auto-opens/binds the review locus once corpus loads
Passes a titleFor callback to Stage
Explicitly disables react-hooks/exhaustive-deps in both effects
standouts: Auto-opens the first reviewable mockup and binds its address so the Stage is never an empty void
Deep-link fix: if a mockup was opened before the corpus loaded, it looks up and binds the address afterward without clobbering an existing user-selected address
Embodies 'click indicates + consents' and 'reflects-never-owns' principles in comments
concerns: react-hooks/exhaustive-deps disabled in both effects, so stale closure or missed dependency bugs are possible
Auto-setting reviewMockup on mount could surprise users if they expected a blank state, though it only fires when none is open
titleFor does a linear corpus.find on every Stage title render; negligible but not memoized
notes: This is the live, corpus-backed Review region that replaces the old standalone STUDIO.html by composing existing StudioKit primitives and binding the real RHM/Composer to the indicated locus.
questions: What is the exact signature of setReviewMockup in AppContext?
How does Stage consume the titleFor callback and render the mockup?
What does RhmPanel do beyond mounting Composer and AddressHelp?
How is reviewAddress updated when the user selects a specific element vs the whole screen?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual code: it is the surface skeleton that composes named primitives, binds the locus from the corpus, and mounts the real RHM panel and Composer.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a first-class, in-app design-review surface with clear structural seams, backend-backed annotations/chat, and principled behavior around locus selection and state ownership.
novelty: Distinct from a standalone mockup-feedback page: it composes existing StudioKit primitives and wires the RHM panel and Composer to a shared, address-keyed annotation store rather than a private jsonl feedback file.
contribution: Provides the Review region that orchestrates corpus-driven locus selection and mounts the Rail, Stage, RhmPanel, and addressed Composer as a real, scoped FE surface.
summary_for_embedding: Review.tsx is a React region that renders a three-pane studio surface (Rail gallery, Stage mockup, RhmPanel with Composer) and auto-binds the first reviewable mockup/address from the corpus.
intention_for_embedding: To turn the design-review studio into a live, corpus-backed in-app region with proper locus binding and right-hand-man feedback.

symbols.does: Looks up the display title for a corpus file path, falling back to the raw path.
symbols.description: A small file-local helper that receives the corpus array and a file identifier, searches for a matching row, and returns the row title or the file string itself when no match exists.

symbols.does: Renders the Review studio region and auto-binds the review locus from the corpus.
symbols.description: The exported React component that refreshes the corpus on mount, opens/binds the first reviewable mockup and its address once corpus data arrives, and renders the studio-shell layout composed of Rail, Stage, RhmPanel, and Composer.
